course_description: Learn programming concepts by controlling a robot on a grid! Built with the 4C/ID instructional design framework. Start with simple moves and work your way up to writing real Python code.

modules.title: Sequences: First Steps
modules.description: Learn to give the robot step-by-step instructions. Each block moves the robot one square on the grid.
modules.concept: Sequencing - putting instructions in the right order

levels.title: Hello, Robot!
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Your First Command

🤖 Imagine a puppy: you say "sit!" and it sits. Your robot is the same — it does exactly what you tell it! Right now it knows one trick: Move Right.

🎯 Your Mission

Guide your robot to the green flag 🏁! Count the squares — that's how many Move Right blocks you need!

🛠️ Sequence Builder

➡️➡️➡️

Each arrow = one block. The robot follows them in order!

📖 How to Do It

1Drag a Move Right block
2Add more Move Right blocks (count the squares!)
3Press Play ▶️ and watch!

💡 Pro Tip

Finger-count the squares on screen before you start. It really helps!

🎮 Fun Fact

Mars rovers get commands just like your robot — step by step from Earth! 🚀

hints: Count the squares from the robot to the flag. How many steps to the right?
You need exactly 3 'Move Right' blocks!

levels.title: Up and Away
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Two Directions

🤖 Giving directions isn't just "go right" — sometimes you say "go up the hill!" Now your robot has Move Up too! It's like chess pieces going different directions.

🎯 Your Mission

The goal is up AND to the right! Combine Move Right and Move Up.

🛠️ Sequence Builder

➡️➡️➡️⬆️⬆️⬆️

Each arrow = one block. The robot follows them in order!

📖 How to Do It

1Drag Move Right blocks to go sideways
2Drag Move Up blocks to go upward
3Press Play ▶️ — blocks run top to bottom!

💡 Pro Tip

Order doesn't have to be perfect! As long as you reach the flag, you win!

🎮 Fun Fact

GPS navigation works the same way — small turns: forward, left, right. Just like your blocks! 🗺

hints: The goal is to the right AND above the robot. You need both directions!
Move right 3 times, then move up 3 times. Or mix them up - both work!

levels.title: Four Directions
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Four Directions!

🤖 A compass has 4 directions. Now your robot does too! With Move Down and Move Left, you can reach ANY square. It's like unlocking all keys on a game controller! 🎮

🎯 Your Mission

The goal is to the left and below. Use Move Left and Move Down to get there!

🛠️ Sequence Builder

⬅️⬅️⬇️⬇️⬇️⬇️

Each arrow = one block. The robot follows them in order!

📖 How to Do It

1Use Move Left to go sideways
2Use Move Down to go lower
3Combine them in any order that reaches the flag!

💡 Pro Tip

Trace the path with your finger first. Count: "2 lefts and 4 downs." Then build it!

🎮 Fun Fact

Mario, Zelda, and Minecraft characters all move with the same 4 directions! 🕹️

hints: The goal is to the left and below the robot.
Move left 2 times, then move down 4 times.

levels.title: Around the Wall
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Navigating Obstacles

🤖 A big puddle blocks the sidewalk. You go AROUND it! Walls work the same — the robot can't walk through them, so plan a detour! 🧱

🎯 Your Mission

A wall blocks the straight path! Find a clear path around it.

🛠️ Sequence Builder

➡️⬆️⬆️➡️➡️➡️⬇️⬇️➡️

Each arrow = one block. The robot follows them in order!

📖 How to Do It

1Spot the wall — it blocks the middle!
2Go UP or DOWN to get around the wall
3Continue heading toward the flag 🏁

💡 Pro Tip

There's often more than one correct path. Can you find the shortest one? 🤔

🎮 Fun Fact

Self-driving cars solve this millions of times per second! 🚗

hints: The wall blocks the direct path. Go around it!
Try: right 1, up 2, right 3, down 2, right 1.

modules.title: Repetition: Loops Save the Day
modules.description: Discover loops - a way to repeat the same action multiple times without writing it over and over.
modules.concept: Loops - repeating actions a set number of times

levels.title: Repeat After Me
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Loops!

🤖 Your teacher says "write this 100 times." Would you? No way! You'd say "repeat 100 times!" That's a loop! 🔄

🎯 Your Mission

Goal is 8 squares right. Instead of 8 blocks (boring! 😴), use ONE Repeat block!

🔎 Loop Unroller

Repeat 8x➡️➡️➡️➡️➡️➡️➡️➡️➡️

One Repeat block = 8 moves! 😎

📖 How to Do It

1Drag a Repeat block & set to 8
2Put Move Right INSIDE the repeat
3Press Play ▶️ — watch the robot zoom!

💡 Pro Tip

If you find yourself repeating the same blocks, think: "Can I use a loop?" The answer is almost always YES!

🎮 Fun Fact

Programmers are proudly lazy — they always find ways to do less work! 😎

hints: The goal is 8 squares to the right. That's a lot of blocks!
Use 'Repeat 8 times' with 'Move Right' inside. Just 2 blocks!

levels.title: Staircase
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Repeating a Pattern

🤖 Climbing stairs: foot up, foot forward. Up, forward. It's a pattern that repeats! You can put MULTIPLE blocks inside a loop! 🪜

🎯 Your Mission

Climb the staircase! Each "stair" is Move Right + Move Up. Loop it!

🔎 Loop Unroller

Repeat 5x➡️➡⬆➡⬆➡⬆➡⬆➡⬆

1 loop with 2 blocks inside = 10 moves! Only 3 blocks total!

📖 How to Do It

1Drag a Repeat 5 block
2Put Move Right + Move Up inside
3Press Play ▶️ — watch the staircase!

💡 Pro Tip

The number of stairs = the number of times to repeat. Count the stair-steps on the grid!

🎮 Fun Fact

Animated movies use loops too! Walking animations repeat the same leg movement over and over. 🎬

hints: The path looks like stairs going up-right. What pattern repeats?
Repeat 5 times: Move Right, Move Up. That's only 3 blocks!

levels.title: Two Loops
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Multiple Loops

🤖 "Walk 3 blocks east, then 4 blocks south." That's TWO loops! Stack them like chapters in a book. 🧭

🎯 Your Mission

The path changes direction! Use two separate loops — one for each section. Watch out for walls!

🔎 Two Loops in Action

Loop 1: 4x⬇️⬇️⬇️⬇️
Loop 2: 5x➡️➡️➡️➡️➡️

📖 How to Do It

1First Repeat for direction 1
2Second Repeat below for direction 2
3Add a third loop if needed!

💡 Pro Tip

Plan each loop separately. "How far this direction?" Then: "How far next?"

🎮 Fun Fact

Music uses this too! Repeat verse 2 times, then chorus 4 times. 🎵

hints: You need to go down first to get past the wall, then right, then down again.
Try: Repeat 4 times Move Down, then Repeat 5 times Move Right, then Repeat 2 times Move Down.

levels.title: The Long Bridge
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Choosing the Right Numbers

🤖 You know loops — now the challenge is the RIGHT numbers! Be a detective: look at clues, count carefully, crack the code! 🔍

🎯 Your Mission

Cross the long bridge! Count squares for each section and set the right loop numbers!

📖 How to Do It

1Study the grid — where are the walls?
2Count squares for each straight section
3Build a loop for each section!

💡 Pro Tip

If the robot bumps into a wall, check which loop has the wrong number. Testing and fixing is normal!

🎮 Fun Fact

Game devs count pixels, web devs count screen sizes — counting is a huge part of real coding! 📐

hints: The wall forces you to go right first along the top, then find a way down.
Go right 9, down 1, then navigate around the wall corner to reach the bottom-right.

modules.title: Patterns: Shapes and Paths
modules.description: Combine loops with direction changes to create cool patterns like L-shapes, zigzags, and spirals.
modules.concept: Pattern recognition - finding repeating patterns in complex paths

levels.title: The L-Shape
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Recognizing Patterns

🤖 A detective looks for patterns. A scientist looks for patterns. A programmer looks for patterns! Pattern recognition is your new superpower! 🦸

🎯 Your Mission

Navigate around the L-shaped wall to reach the goal!

🛠️ Sequence Builder

⬇️➡️➡️➡️➡️➡️⬇️⬇️⬇️⬇️

Each arrow = one block. The robot follows them in order!

📖 How to Do It

1See the walls — they form an L-shape!
2Your path goes AROUND the L
3Use loops for the straight parts

💡 Pro Tip

Draw the path in your mind first. Each straight section = one loop!

🎮 Fun Fact

Tetris pieces are all made of patterns! L-piece, T-piece, Z-piece — programmers use pattern recognition to make the game work! 🧩

hints: The walls form an L-shape. You need to go around them in an L-shape too!
Go right 5, down 5 - but the walls are in the way. Try down 1 first, then right, then down.

levels.title: Zigzag Road
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Zigzag Patterns

🤖 A skier slaloming down a mountain: left, right, left, right — that's a zigzag! Find the smallest piece that repeats and put it in a loop! ⛷️

🎯 Your Mission

Walls force a zigzag path! Find the repeating pattern and loop it to reach the goal!

🔄 Zigzag Pattern

⬇⬇➡⬆⬆➡🔄⬇⬇➡⬆⬆➡

See the pattern? Down-Right-Up-Right repeats!

📖 How to Do It

1Trace the path through the zigzag with your finger
2Find the smallest piece that repeats
3Put that piece inside a Repeat block!

💡 Pro Tip

The smallest repeating piece might be 2 or 4 blocks. Try different combos!

🎮 Fun Fact

Lightning bolts and mountain roads are all zigzags! Scientists call this a "serpentine pattern." 🐍

hints: The walls create a zigzag path. You go down, then right, then up, then right... see the pattern?
Repeat 4 times: Move Down 2, Move Right 1, Move Up 2, Move Right 1. But adjust for the exact layout!

levels.title: The Spiral
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Spiral Patterns

🤖 A spiral is like a zigzag's cool big sibling! Think of a snail's shell 🐌 or water going down a drain. Each side gets shorter as you spiral inward!

🎯 Your Mission

Follow the spiral corridor from the outside to the center!

🌀 Spiral Shape

🔵Right 🟢Down 🔴Left — each side needs its own loop!

📖 How to Do It

1Go right along the top edge (loop!)
2Go down the right edge (loop!)
3Go left along the bottom (loop!)
4Keep spiraling inward to the center!

💡 Pro Tip

Count each side of the spiral separately. Write each side as its own loop!

🎮 Fun Fact

Galaxies, hurricanes, and even DNA are all spirals! 🌀

hints: Follow the spiral corridor: right along the top, down the right side, left along bottom, up into the center.
Go right 6, down 6, left 6, up 4, right 2, down 2 to reach the goal.

modules.title: Collecting Items: Pick It Up!
modules.description: Learn to use the pick_up command to collect stars and treasures scattered around the grid.
modules.concept: Sequential actions - combining movement with other actions

levels.title: Star Collector
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Picking Up Items

🤖 Your robot got an upgrade — it can pick things up! ⭐ Walk over an item, then grab it! Like collecting coins in a video game.

🎯 Your Mission

Stars are along the path! Walk to each one and pick it up. Collect ALL of them! ⭐⭐⭐

🛠️ Sequence Builder

➡️➡️➡️➡️➡️

Each arrow = one block. The robot follows them in order!

➡️⭐Pick Up!➡️➡️⭐Pick Up!➡️➡️⭐Pick Up!

📖 How to Do It

1Use Move Right to walk to the first star
2Use Pick Up when standing on the star
3Keep moving and picking up!

💡 Pro Tip

The robot must be ON the star to pick it up. Move FIRST, then pick up!

🎮 Fun Fact

In Super Mario, coins appear in lines and Mario collects them the same way! 🍄

hints: Walk to each star and pick it up! Move right, pick up, move right, move right, pick up...
Right, Pick Up, Right, Right, Pick Up, Right, Right, Pick Up.

levels.title: Treasure Hunt
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Planning a Collection Route

🤖 You're a treasure hunter now! 🗺️ When treasures are scattered, plan the best route to collect them all. Some paths are shorter than others!

🎯 Your Mission

Five stars scattered across the grid with walls! Plan a route to collect ALL of them!

📖 How to Do It

1Look at where ALL the stars are
2Plan a route that visits each star
3Use loops for long straight sections!

💡 Pro Tip

Start by collecting stars that are closest together. Don't zigzag back and forth!

🎮 Fun Fact

Amazon and UPS use algorithms to plan delivery routes — the same puzzle you're solving! 📦

hints: Plan a route that picks up all 5 stars. Try going right along the top first.
Right 3 + Pick Up, Right 2 + Pick Up, Down 3 + Pick Up, Down 2 + Pick Up, Left 5 + Pick Up.

levels.title: Row by Row
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Collecting in a Pattern

🤖 Stars in a neat pattern? Use LOOPS to collect them super efficiently! It's like mowing a lawn — go across one row, move down, come back. 🎯

🎯 Your Mission

Stars are in three rows! Go across each row collecting, then move to the next. Like reading a book — left to right, then down!

🔎 Loop Unroller

Repeat 3x➡️➡➡⭐➡➡⭐➡➡⭐

Each loop cycle: Move Right, Move Right, Pick Up!

📖 How to Do It

1Row 1: Loop to move right and pick up each star
2Move down to Row 2
3Row 2: Loop going LEFT this time (zigzag!)
4Move down to Row 3 and repeat!

💡 Pro Tip

Look at star spacing. If stars are every 2 squares, your loop is: Right, Right, Pick Up!

🎮 Fun Fact

Printers work like this! The print head goes left-to-right, drops down, then right-to-left. 🖨️

hints: The stars form a zigzag pattern across three rows. Go right across row 1, then down, then left across row 2, then down, then right across row 3.
Row 1: Repeat 3 times (Right, Right, Pick Up). Then Down 2, Pick Up. Row 2: Repeat 3 times (Left, Left, Pick Up). Then Down 2, Pick Up. Row 3: same as Row 1.

levels.title: Warehouse Run
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Combining Everything!

🤖 Welcome to the warehouse! 🏝 Navigate a complex space, avoid obstacles, and collect all packages. Every skill you've learned comes together!

🎯 Your Mission

Collect all 5 items scattered across a warehouse full of walls! Plan your route carefully.

📖 How to Do It

1Scan the grid: where are items? Where are walls?
2Plan a route visiting all items
3Build with moves, pick-ups, and loops
4Test it — press Play and watch!

💡 Pro Tip

Use the 5-step method: Scan, Plan, Simplify, Code, Test!

🎮 Fun Fact

Amazon warehouses have real robots zipping around collecting items — using the SAME concepts! 🤖📦

hints: There are 5 items to collect. Plan a route that avoids the two wall sections.
One possible route: right to first item, continue right to top-right item, down to bottom-right, left along bottom to bottom-left, then navigate to the center item.

modules.title: Conditionals: Smart Decisions
modules.description: Teach the robot to make decisions! Use 'if' blocks to check for walls and items before acting.
modules.concept: Conditional logic - making decisions based on what the robot sees

levels.title: Wall Detector
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: IF Decisions!

🤖 Until now, your robot followed instructions blindly. Now it can think! 🧠 The If block checks something and makes a decision. Like: "IF there's a puddle, THEN step around it."

🎯 Your Mission

A wall blocks the path! Use If Wall Ahead to detect it and go around!

🫶 Decision Tree

Wall ahead?
✅ YES
Go around it!
❌ NO
Keep going right

📖 How to Do It

1Move right toward the goal
2Use If Wall Ahead before hitting the wall
3Inside the If: tell the robot to go around
4Continue moving right to the goal!

💡 Pro Tip

The If block only runs its inside code when the condition is TRUE. No wall? It just skips!

🎮 Fun Fact

"If-then" logic is the foundation of ALL programs! Every game: "IF player presses jump, THEN jump." 🏆

hints: Use If Wall Ahead to detect the wall, then go around it.
Move Right 3 times. If Wall Ahead, Move Up, Move Right, Move Down. Then Move Right twice more.

levels.title: Item Scanner
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Auto Item Scanner

🤖 The If Item Here block gives your robot eyes! It checks: "Is there an item here?" If yes, pick it up! No need to memorize locations! ⚡

🎯 Your Mission

Items are scattered along the path. Use a loop with If Item Here to automatically scan and collect!

🫶 Decision Tree

Item here?
✅ YES
Pick it up! ⭐
❌ NO
Keep moving

📖 How to Do It

1Drag a Repeat block (set to grid width)
2Inside: put Move Right first
3Then: If Item Here ➡ Pick Up
4The robot walks and auto-collects!

💡 Pro Tip

The loop-with-check combo is incredibly powerful. Use it whenever you need to do something "if the situation is right."

🎮 Fun Fact

Roomba vacuums work this way! Drive around, IF dirt detected, THEN vacuum harder. Same logic! 🤖

hints: Instead of remembering where each item is, use 'If Item Here' to automatically pick up!
Repeat 7 times: Move Right, If Item Here then Pick Up. Only 4 blocks!

levels.title: Smart Navigator
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Multiple Conditions

🤖 One condition is good. TWO conditions are awesome! 💪 Check for walls AND items at the same time. Plus the NOT block flips true/false!

🎯 Your Mission

Navigate through walls AND collect items! Use both If Wall Ahead and If Item Here.

🫶 Double Decision

Wall ahead?
✅ Dodge!
❌ Go right
Item here?
✅ Pick up!
❌ Ignore

📖 How to Do It

1Start moving right toward the goal
2Use If Wall Ahead to dodge walls
3Use If Item Here to grab items
4Combine both checks in a loop!

💡 Pro Tip

Check for items BEFORE you move away, and check for walls BEFORE you walk into them. Order matters!

🎮 Fun Fact

Self-driving cars use HUNDREDS of conditions at once! Your two conditions are the beginning of that technology! 🚗

hints: Move right across the top row. When you hit a wall, go down, right, and back up. Check for items along the way!
Use a loop with 'If Wall Ahead' to dodge walls, and 'If Item Here' to collect items as you go.

levels.title: Minefield
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Think Like a Programmer

🤖 This is a real brain teaser! 🧩 Walls everywhere, items scattered around. An algorithm is a step-by-step plan — and that's what professional programmers create every day!

🎯 Your Mission

Navigate walls, collect 4 items, and reach the goal at the bottom-right corner!

📖 How to Do It

1Map it out: look at all walls and items first
2Plan your route: visit each item while avoiding walls
3Use conditions: If Wall Ahead + If Item Here
4Build and test: try it, then fix mistakes!

💡 Pro Tip

Draw the route on paper before coding! Even professional programmers sketch ideas first.

🎮 Fun Fact

NASA programmers planned the Mars rover's route exactly like this! You're basically training for NASA! 🚀

hints: Plan your route on paper first! Visit all 4 items and reach the goal at bottom-right.
One route: Down 2 (pick up), Down 2, Right 2, Up 3 (pick up at 4,1), Down 3, Right 2 (pick up), Down 2, Right 2, Up 3 (pick up), Down 3.

modules.title: While Loops: Until You Get There
modules.description: Learn while loops - loops that keep going until a condition is met. The robot keeps moving until it reaches the goal!
modules.concept: While loops - repeating until a condition changes

levels.title: Walk Until You Arrive
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: While Loops!

🤖 Repeat is great when you know the exact number. But what if you DON'T? While loop = "keep going until you get there!" Like walking to school without counting steps. 🤔

🎯 Your Mission

Use While Not At Goal to make the robot walk until it reaches the flag. No counting needed! 🏁

🔄 Repeat vs While

Repeat 7x
You MUST count 😬
While Not At Goal
Auto-stops! 😎

📖 How to Do It

1Drag While Not At Goal
2Put Move Right INSIDE
3That's it! Just 2 blocks! Press Play ▶️

💡 Pro Tip

While loops never stop until the job is done. But careful: if the goal is unreachable, it runs forever! (That's an "infinite loop")

🎮 Fun Fact

Video games use while loops constantly: "While player is alive, keep game running." 🎮

hints: You don't need to count the squares! Just use 'While Not At Goal' with 'Move Right'.
Only 2 blocks needed: While Not At Goal > Move Right.

levels.title: Wall Dodger
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: While + If = Smart Robot!

🤖 Combine While with If and your robot becomes truly intelligent! Like riding a bike: keep pedaling (while) and when you see an obstacle, steer around it (if). 🌟

🎯 Your Mission

Walls in the path! Use While Not At Goal with If Wall Ahead inside. The robot dodges walls automatically!

🫶 Decision Tree

Wall ahead?
✅ YES
Move Down ⬇️
❌ NO
Move Right ➡️

📖 How to Do It

1Drag While Not At Goal as the outer block
2Inside: If Wall Ahead ➡ Move Down
3Also inside: Move Right (after the If)
4Press Play ▶️ — auto-dodging!

💡 Pro Tip

The If goes INSIDE the While. Every loop cycle: check for walls, dodge if needed, then move right.

🎮 Fun Fact

This is a real algorithm called "wall following!" Maze-solving robots use this exact technique. 🏆

hints: Use 'While Not At Goal' with an 'If Wall Ahead' check inside.
While Not At Goal: If Wall Ahead then Move Down, Move Right. Just 4 blocks!

levels.title: The Greedy Collector
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Multi-Action While Loop

🤖 Your while loop does THREE things every step: check for items, check for walls, and move. It's called a "greedy algorithm" — grab whatever you can at every step! 🍬

🎯 Your Mission

Items and walls in a staircase pattern. One while loop handles BOTH collecting AND dodging!

⚙️ Loop Body Order

1👀 Check feet: If Item ➡ Pick Up
2👀 Look ahead: If Wall ➡ Move Down
3🚶 Step forward: Move Right

📖 How to Do It

1While Not At Goal (main loop)
2Inside: If Item Here ➡ Pick Up
3Inside: If Wall Ahead ➡ Move Down
4Inside: Move Right (keep moving!)

💡 Pro Tip

ORDER MATTERS! Check items first (before you walk away), then walls (before you crash), then move.

🎮 Fun Fact

Roomba vacuums use exactly this pattern: while battery alive ➡ check dirt, check walls, keep moving. 🤖

hints: The items and walls form a staircase pattern. Collect items and dodge walls as you descend.
While Not At Goal: If Item Here then Pick Up, If Wall Ahead then Move Down, Move Right. 6 blocks total!

levels.title: Infinite Hallway
levels.theory:
🌟 New Skill: Algorithms That Adapt

🤖 You're writing a program that works on a maze you've never seen! 🧙 The algorithm adapts to whatever it finds — this is real programming power!

🎯 Your Mission

A complex staircase maze with walls and items. Write ONE algorithm that navigates the whole thing!

📖 How to Do It

1While Not At Goal as the outer loop
2If Item Here ➡ Pick Up inside
3If Wall Ahead ➡ Move Down inside
4Move Right at the bottom of the loop
5Add extra moves for the final stretch!

💡 Pro Tip

If your robot gets stuck at the end, add a few extra moves AFTER the while loop.

🎮 Fun Fact

This "general solution" approach is what makes AI powerful. Instead of coding every situation, write algorithms that adapt! 🤯

hints: The maze has a staircase pattern. Move right, and when blocked, go down and continue right.
While Not At Goal: If Item Here Pick Up, If Wall Ahead Move Down, Move Right. Then handle the final descent.

modules.title: Complex Mazes: Master Challenge
modules.description: Put everything together to solve challenging mazes that require sequences, loops, conditionals, and collecting items.
modules.concept: Algorithm design - combining all concepts to solve complex problems

levels.title: The Snake Path
levels.theory:
🌟 Master Challenge: The Snake Path

🤖 Time to become a true coding hero! 🦸 This uses EVERYTHING: sequences, loops, conditions, and item collection. The path winds like a snake through corridors!

🎯 Your Mission

Navigate the snake-shaped maze! Right across a corridor, drop down through a gap, left across, drop down, right again... collect items in the middle!

🐍 Snake Pattern

➡️ right
⬇️ drop
⬅️ left
⬇️ drop
➡️ right

📖 How to Do It

1Go RIGHT across the top corridor (loop!)
2Drop DOWN through the gap
3Go LEFT across the next corridor
4Drop DOWN again and go RIGHT
5Collect items with Pick Up along the way!

💡 Pro Tip

The snake pattern repeats! Right-drop-left-drop. Look for how many cycles you need.

🎮 Fun Fact

The game "Snake" uses this exact pattern. You're basically programming Snake! 🐍

hints: The path snakes: right across the top, down through the gap, left across, down through gap, right across, down, right to goal.
Pattern: go right to end of row, drop down through the gap, go left to end, drop down, repeat. Collect items in the middle!

levels.title: The Treasure Vault
levels.theory:
🌟 Master Challenge: The Treasure Vault

🤖 Like a dungeon in a fantasy game! 🏰 Multiple rooms with treasure inside, connected by doorways. The key skill: decomposition — breaking a big problem into smaller pieces.

🎯 Your Mission

The grid is divided into rooms by walls. Each room has items! Find doorways, explore every room, grab every treasure!

📖 How to Do It

1Room 1: Collect items in the first area
2Find the door: Look for gaps in walls
3Room 2: Collect items in the next area
4Keep going through doors until all items collected!
5Navigate to the goal in the last room!

💡 Pro Tip

Solve each room as its own mini-puzzle. Then connect solutions. This "divide and conquer" is how pros tackle huge projects!

🎮 Fun Fact

Zelda and Minecraft dungeons work exactly like this — rooms with treasures connected by doors! 🎮

hints: Collect items in the left rooms first, then pass through the gap at row 3 to the right side.
Use the gap at column 5 row 4 to enter the bottom area and reach the goal.

levels.title: The Grand Maze
levels.theory:
🌟 Master Challenge: The Grand Maze

🤖 The ULTIMATE block programming challenge! 🏆 You need every skill plus computational thinking — the 4-step superpower of all computer scientists!

🎯 Your Mission

Conquer the grand maze! Navigate multiple wall sections, collect 7 items, and reach the goal. After this, you're ready for Python! 🐍

🧠 4 Steps of Computational Thinking

🔍
Decompose
Break into sections
🔎
Patterns
Find repeating parts
💡
Abstract
General solutions
⚙️
Algorithm
Put it all together

📖 How to Do It

1Decompose: Divide maze into sections between wall columns
2Navigate: Find the gap in each wall
3Collect: Grab items as you pass through
4Combine: Use loops, conditions, and sequences together!

💡 Pro Tip

Don't try to collect EVERY item in one pass. Focus on reaching the goal first!

🎮 Fun Fact

After this level, you've mastered the same thinking skills that CS students learn in college! 🎓

hints: Break the maze into sections. Navigate through gaps in the vertical walls at columns 1, 3, 5, and 7.
Don't try to collect every item in one pass. Plan a route that gets most items while making progress toward the bottom-right goal.

modules.title: Python Mode: Write Real Code
modules.description: Translate everything you learned into Python - a real programming language used by professionals!
modules.concept: Text-based programming - writing the same logic in Python code

levels.title: Python: First Steps
levels.theory:
🌟 Python: Your First Real Code!

🤖 Welcome to the big leagues! 🎉 Same logic as blocks, but now you TYPE commands. Like switching from picture books to chapter books — the stories are the same!

🎯 Your Mission

Guide the robot to the goal using Python! Type robot.move_right() for each step.

# Your first Python code!
robot.move_right()
robot.move_right()
robot.move_right()

📖 How to Do It

1Type robot.move_right() on each line
2Each line = one step for the robot
3Press Run ▶️ and watch your code come alive!

💡 Pro Tip

Every command starts with robot. and ends with (). Don't forget the parentheses!

🎮 Fun Fact

Python is used by Google, NASA, Netflix, and Instagram! And it was named after Monty Python comedy show! 🐍

hints: Type robot.move_right() five times, each on a new line.
Remember: each command needs robot. at the start and () at the end!

levels.title: Python: For Loops
levels.theory:
🌟 Python: For Loops

🤖 The Repeat block becomes a for loop! Type for i in range(5): to repeat 5 times. Same idea, real code! 🔄

🎯 Your Mission

The goal is far away diagonally! Write a for loop to climb the staircase. One loop, two moves inside!

# Staircase loop
for i in range(7):
robot.move_right()
robot.move_up()

📖 How to Do It

1Type for i in range(7): (colon is important!)
2Indent 4 spaces + robot.move_right()
3Indent 4 spaces + robot.move_up()
4Press Run ▶️ — staircase!

💡 Pro Tip

The number in range() is how many times to repeat. Both indented lines repeat together!

🎮 Fun Fact

Python is famous for indentation. Its motto: "Beautiful is better than ugly!" ✨

hints: You need to go right 7 and up 7. Use a for loop to make a staircase!
for i in range(7):\n    robot.move_right()\n    robot.move_up()

levels.title: Python: If Statements
levels.theory:
🌟 Python: If Statements

🤖 The If block becomes if robot.is_wall_ahead(): in Python. While becomes while not robot.is_at_goal():. Same logic, text form! 📝

🎯 Your Mission

Write Python that navigates through walls AND collects items — automatically!

while not robot.is_at_goal():
if robot.is_item_here():
robot.pick_up()
if robot.is_wall_ahead():
robot.move_down()
else:
robot.move_right()

📖 How to Do It

1Type while not robot.is_at_goal():
2Indent: item check + pick up
3Indent: wall check + move down
4Indent: else + move right

💡 Pro Tip

The else keyword runs when if is FALSE. Like a fork in the road!

🎮 Fun Fact

The while+if combo is the foundation of artificial intelligence! 🤖🧠

hints: Write a while loop that moves right, picks up items, and goes down when blocked.

levels.title: Python: The Final Challenge
levels.theory:
🌟 Python: The Final Challenge!

🤖 You're now a Python programmer! 🎓 For the finale: functions! A function is your own custom block. Write def dodge_wall(): and call it anywhere! 💃

🎯 Your Mission

The ultimate maze! Navigate 4 wall sections, collect 7 items, and reach the goal. Use for loops, while loops, if/else, and custom functions!

# Define your own function!
def dodge_wall():
robot.move_down()
robot.move_right()
robot.move_right()
robot.move_up()
# Now use it!
dodge_wall() # so clean!

🏆 Skills Unlocked!

➡️ Sequences🔄 Loops🫶 Conditions⭐ Items🐍 Python🛠️ Functions

📖 How to Do It

1Define helper functions for repeated patterns
2Use while not robot.is_at_goal(): as main loop
3Add if checks for walls and items
4Use for loops for straight sections
5Add # comments to explain your thinking!

💡 Pro Tip

Add comments with # to explain your code. Good comments explain WHY, not what.

🎮 Fun Fact

You just went from zero to Python programmer! These skills work in EVERY language: Java, JavaScript, C++. 🌟🚀🎉

hints: Break the maze into vertical sections separated by walls at columns 2, 4, 6, and 8. Find the gaps to pass through.
Use a combination of for loops for straight sections and if statements for wall detection. Define helper functions for repeated patterns!
